name: graphql
context: GitHub GraphQL API v4 тАФ schema exploration, queries, mutations, and connections
info: The GitHub GraphQL API v4 provides a single endpoint (`https://api.github.com/graphql`) for deeply nested, efficient queries across GitHub resources. Unlike [[github/rest]] where you may need multiple round-trips, GraphQL lets you fetch exactly the fields you need in a single request. It supports introspection for schema discovery, connections for paginated relationships, mutations for write operations, and fragments for reusable field selections. Rate limiting uses a point-based system (5,000 points/hour). See [[github/auth]] for authentication, [[github/rate-limits]] for point calculations, and [[github/pagination]] for cursor-based traversal.

sections.title: Queries and variables
sections.body: Queries read data. Use variables (`$owner: String!`) for parameterized, reusable queries. Pass variables as a separate JSON object alongside the query string. Use aliases to fetch the same field with different arguments in a single query. Always request only the fields you need to minimize response size and rate limit cost.

sections.title: Mutations
sections.body: Mutations create, update, or delete resources. They take typed input objects (`CreateIssueInput`, `AddCommentInput`) and return the modified resource. Common mutations: `createIssue`, `addComment`, `mergePullRequest`, `createPullRequest`, `addReaction`. Mutation inputs often require node IDs тАФ fetch these via queries first.

sections.title: Connections and pagination
sections.body: Relationships use the Relay connection pattern with `first`/`after` (forward) or `last`/`before` (backward) arguments. Connections return `pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }` and either `nodes` (flat list) or `edges` (with cursor per item). Maximum `first`/`last` value is 100. See [[github/pagination]] for traversal patterns.

sections.title: Schema introspection
sections.body: Use `__schema` and `__type` queries to explore available types, fields, and arguments at runtime. The GraphQL Explorer provides an interactive IDE with autocomplete and documentation. Introspection is essential for discovering node IDs, available mutations, and connection arguments.

sections.title: Rate limit cost calculation
sections.body: Each query costs points based on the number of nodes it could return. A query requesting `first: 100` on a connection costs 100 points regardless of actual results. Nested connections multiply: `first: 100` issues each with `first: 10` labels costs 100 + (100 * 10) = 1,100 points. Query the `rateLimit` field to check cost and remaining budget. See [[github/rate-limits]].

links.context: REST API v3 as an alternative to GraphQL

related.note: REST v3 is simpler for single-resource CRUD; GraphQL excels at nested data
A Bearer token is required for all GraphQL requests
GraphQL uses point-based rate limiting, not request counting
GraphQL uses cursor-based pagination via the Relay connection pattern

author: dojo-community
